description_en: A haiku monument (kuhi) commemorating Basho at Eijuji, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1984-09-11.

name_romaji: Takahashi Dōsai Sen Narabini Sho ni yoru Chōsei Dōshi no Hi
name_en: Chosei Doshi Monument Composed and Brushed by Takahashi Dosai
description_en: A stone monument for Chosei Doshi, composed and brushed by Takahashi Dosai, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1988-03-22.
The eleven-faced Kannon statue of Fushimiyama, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 1979-06-29.

name_en: Arafune Cold Storage Site for Silkworm Eggs
description_en: The site of the Arafune wind-cave cold storage for silkworm eggs (sanshu), designated as a historic site. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 2006-11-29.

name_en: Onigasawa Bridge of the Kozuke Railway
description_en: The Onigasawa bridge of the Kozuke Railway, classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 2006-11-29.
The Shunjukan documents (monjo), classified under tangible cultural properties. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 2013-01-23.

name_en: Excavated Artifacts from the Shimokamada-Somase Site
description_en: Artifacts excavated from the Shimokamada-Somase site, designated as a historic site. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 2016-03-18.
The natural colony of skunk cabbage (zazenso) at Kuwamoto, designated as a natural monument. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 2016-03-18.
The site of the Shunjukan, designated as a historic site. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture.

name_en: Stone Rod Excavated from the Hattoya Site
description_en: A stone rod (sekibo) excavated from the Hattoya site, designated as a historic site. Town-designated by Shimonita, Gunma Prefecture, on 2019-03-19.

The Kitayama Chausuyama ancient burial mound (kofun), designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1971-04-10. Located at 99-1 Minamigoka and Kamitakase 1817/1799.
The Hachimangu shrine at Yokose, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1972-02-08. Located at 864 Kamitakase.

The Taishidozuka, also called Tabinezuka, ancient burial mound (kofun), designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1974-05-13. Located at 245-1, 245-2 and 246 Ichinomiya.

name_en: Basho Haiku Monument with Unribo Haiku Monument
description_en: A haiku monument (kuhi) commemorating Basho, with an attached Unribo haiku monument, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1974-05-13. Located at 245-1 Ichinomiya.
A portrait stone tablet (gazo itabi) of Seiunji, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1974-05-13. Located at 1839 Tomioka.

A stone tablet (itabi) bearing a Karyaku-era inscription, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1975-04-30. Located at 1841 Nogami-otsu.

name_romaji: Mokuchō Amida Nyorai Zazō Tsuketari Kondōsei Jūichimen Kannon Kakebotoke
name_en: Wooden Seated Amida Nyorai with Gilt-Bronze Eleven-Faced Kannon Hanging Icon
description_en: A wooden seated statue of Amida Nyorai with an attached gilt-bronze eleven-faced Kannon hanging icon (kakebotoke), classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1977-03-07. Located at 227 Ichinomiya.
A pair of ritual cymbals (nyohachi), classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1977-03-07. Located at 731 Shimotakase.
A statue of the Kannon Bodhisattva (Kanzeon), classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1978-05-27. Located at 1193 Tomioka.
A standing statue of Amida Nyorai, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1978-05-27. Located at 1193 Tomioka.

name_romaji: Satō Tan'un no Bohi Tsuketari Tan'un Jigazō・Suzuri
name_en: Tomb Marker of Sato Tan'un with Self-Portrait and Inkstone
description_en: The tomb marker of Sato Tan'un, with an attached self-portrait and inkstone (suzuri), designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1979-03-28. Located at 2625 Nogami.

name_romaji: Orihon Dai Hannya Haramitta Kyō (Maki Dai Sanbyaku Yonjūni・Maki Dai Sanbyaku Yonjūroku)
name_en: Folded Great Perfection of Wisdom Sutra (scrolls 342 and 346)
description_en: A folded-book (orihon) copy of the Great Perfection of Wisdom Sutra, scrolls 342 and 346, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1980-04-22. Located at 841 Nanokaichi.
A Buddhist statue in the Mokujiki tradition, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1980-04-22. Located at 1568 Shimonyu.

A stone tablet (itabi) bearing a Shoka-era inscription, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1981-03-27. Located at 1454 Shimokuroiwa.

name_romaji: Enkū Saku Jūichimen Senju Kannon Ryūzō
name_en: Standing Eleven-Faced Thousand-Armed Kannon by Enku
description_en: A standing statue of the eleven-faced thousand-armed Kannon carved by Enku, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1981-05-26. Located at Fudodo hall, 596 Kurokawa.
The Doyama Inari ancient burial mound (kofun), designated as a historic site. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture. Located at 115 and 116 Ichinomiya.
The great cedar tree (osugi) of Miyazaki Shrine, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture. Located at 547 Miyazaki.

name_en: Standing Stone Statue of Jizo Bodhisattva
description_en: A standing stone statue of the Jizo Bodhisattva, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1985-01-24. Located at 1187 Minamigoka.
The sudajii (Castanopsis) oak tree of Nukisaki Shrine, designated as a natural monument. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1985-06-25. Located at 1535 Ichinomiya.

name_en: Bronze Mirror with Four Deformed Beasts
description_en: A bronze mirror decorated with four deformed beast motifs (henkei shiju kyo), classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1986-10-20. Held at the municipal art museum, 351-1 Kurokawa.

A catfish-tail (namazuo) helmet with an attached guardian deity, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1986-10-20. On deposit at the municipal art museum, 351-1 Kurokawa.

name_en: Wooden Seated Statue of Kokuzo Bodhisattva
description_en: A wooden seated statue of the Kokuzo Bodhisattva, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1986-10-20. Located at 211 Nakazawa.

The main hall (honden) of Sugawara Shrine, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1988-03-26. Located at 1423 Sugawara, Myogimachi.
The main hall (honden) of Kota Shrine, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1988-03-26. Located at 661 Shimotakada, Myogimachi.

A stone monument bearing a goddess image (megamizo-hi), classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1988-03-26. Located at 985-1 Sugawara, Myogimachi.

A stone monument depicting the eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1988-03-26. Located at 985-1 Sugawara, Myogimachi.

A stone tablet (itabi), classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1988-03-26. Held at the Tomioka municipal folk museum, 1209-13 Kamitakada, Myogimachi.

A stone monument depicting the Jizo Bodhisattva, classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1988-03-26. Located at Doya, Yagirentsure, Myogimachi.
A stone tablet (itabi), classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1988-03-26. Located at 441 Shimotakada, Myogimachi.

name_en: Wooden Standing Statue of Bato Kannon
description_en: A wooden standing statue of Bato Kannon (Horse-Headed Kannon), classified under tangible cultural properties. Municipally designated by Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture, on 1989-03-24. Located at Fujiki Kannon hall, 249 Fujiki.
